title: 10 best herbal decoctions for eye bags
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for eye bags are a natural and effective way to reduce puffiness, dark circles, and fatigue around the eyes. By steeping herbs such as green tea, chamomile, and cucumber in hot water, one can create a soothing liquid that cools and calms the skin. This helps to constrict blood vessels, reducing swelling and discoloration. For example, drinking a warm cup of chamomile decoction before bed can improve sleep quality, leading to reduced eye bags and a refreshed appearance the next morning.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree decoctions helps with eye bags because of its remarkable anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The decoction's active compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, work to reduce puffiness and dark circles by constricting blood vessels and neutralizing free radicals that cause oxidative stress. As a result, the decoction effectively soothes and calms the delicate skin around the eyes, leaving it looking smoother, brighter, and more radiant. Regular use can also help to reduce the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles, further enhancing its rejuvenating benefits.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Maidenhair tree decoctions contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help reduce inflammation in the orbital fat, which can alleviate puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.

Antioxidant property: The high concentration of antioxidants in maidenhair tree decoctions helps protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the appearance of fine lines, wrinkles, and discoloration.
Constricting property: The tannins present in maidenhair tree decoctions can help constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid retention, which can minimize the appearance of eye bags and dark circles.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids (specifically, Quercetin and Isorhapontigenin): These flavonoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce puffiness and swelling associated with eye bags.
Bilobalide: As a sesquiterpene triterpene, bilobalide has neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate eye strain and reduce the appearance of eye bags.

Ginkgolides (specifically, Ginkgolide B and Ginkgolide A): These ginkgolides have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that may contribute to eye bags.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties to reduce puffiness and improve circulation under the eyes.
Barks: Utilized for their flavonoids and terpenoids content, which help to reduce inflammation and improve the appearance of eye bags.
Buds: Employed due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which may help to reduce puffiness and improve skin elasticity around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Measure and combine 2-3 teaspoons of dried ginkgo biloba leaves with 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes in a covered container to allow for optimal extraction.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Add honey or other sweetener to taste before serving the decoction immediately.
Discard the solids and store the remaining decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower decoctions helps with eye bags because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The decoction's active compounds, such as echinacein and isobutylamides, work together to reduce puffiness and dark circles by constricting blood vessels and minimizing fluid retention. Additionally, the herbal extract's soothing and calming effects help to relax the eye area, reducing the appearance of under-eye bags and leaving the skin feeling smooth and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Echinacea's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce puffiness and swelling associated with eye bags by reducing inflammation in the delicate skin around the eyes.

Antioxidant properties: Kansas Coneflower's rich antioxidant content can help protect the skin from free radical damage, which may contribute to the formation of dark circles and eye bags under the eyes.

Antimicrobial properties: Echinacea's antimicrobial compounds may help prevent bacterial or fungal infections that can cause puffiness and swelling in the eyes, making it a potential natural remedy for addressing eye bag-related issues.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds may help reduce puffiness and inflammation in the eye area by inhibiting the release of histamine, a chemical that contributes to swelling.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly kaempferol, found in Echinacea angustifolia, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce eye bag inflammation and protect the skin from oxidative stress.

Alkylamides: These compounds have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce swelling and puffiness in the eye area, making them a potential contributor to eye bag relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: Echinacea angustifolia roots are commonly used due to their rich content of bioactive compounds that aid in reducing puffiness and inflammation.
Leaves: The leaves of Echinacea angustifolia are used in decoctions to help in reducing water retention and swelling associated with eye bags.
Roots: Echinacea angustifolia roots are also used due to their potential to reduce dark circles and promote overall eye health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea angustifolia roots in autumn after the first frost has killed the above-ground plant parts.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm dry place or using a dehydrator at 40 degrees celsius for 6 hours.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and measure out 2 to 4 grams per 250 milliliters of water.
Steep the chopped root pieces in boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the decoction and discard the solids to obtain a clear liquid ready for consumption or storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold decoctions helps with eye bags because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties that effectively reduce puffiness and swelling. The decoction's antioxidant-rich compounds, such as lutein and zeaxanthin, also help to brighten and soothe the under-eye area, reducing discoloration and dark circles. Additionally, the astringent properties of pot marigold can constrict blood vessels, further reducing puffiness and leaving the eyes looking refreshed and revitalized. This natural remedy provides a gentle and effective solution for tackling tired-looking eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Calendula, the herb used to make pot marigold decoction, contains flavonoids and terpenes that reduce inflammation and swelling around the eyes, helping to alleviate eye bags.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in pot marigold decoctions help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to puffiness and discoloration.
Antibacterial properties: Calendula has antibacterial properties that help prevent infections and reduce the occurrence of eye irritations, redness, and swelling associated with eye bags.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins help to reduce inflammation and swelling associated with eye bags by soothing and calming the skin, thereby reducing puffiness.

Flavonoids (including kaempferol and quercetin): Flavonoids exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the appearance of eye bags by protecting the skin from oxidative damage and reducing inflammation.

Triterpenoids (including calendulic acid): Triterpenoids have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to reduce redness and swelling associated with eye bags, promoting a more even and rested appearance of the under-eye area.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help to reduce puffiness and ease eye strain.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage and irritation.
Stems: The stems are used due to their ability to promote circulation and reduce inflammation, which can help to alleviate eye bag symptoms and promote overall eye health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25-30 fresh calendula officinalis flowers at dawn when the petals are fully open and dry them immediately.
Combine 1 tablespoon of dried calendula flowers with 250 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Reduce heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbs to infuse.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Store the cooled calendula decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days in an airtight glass container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe decoctions helps with eye bags because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the delicate skin under the eyes. The herbal ingredients in aloe decoctions work to reduce puffiness and swelling, leaving the area looking smoother and more radiant. Additionally, aloe's antioxidant-rich composition helps protect the skin from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals, promoting a healthy and youthful appearance around the eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce swelling and puffiness under the eyes, alleviating the appearance of eye bags.

Antioxidant properties: Aloe vera's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and inflammation in the delicate skin around the eyes, helping to reduce dark circles and eye bags.

Hydrating and soothing effects: Aloe vera's moisturizing and soothing properties help to hydrate the under-eye area, reducing the appearance of fine lines, wrinkles, and discoloration, while also calming and relaxing the skin.
remedy_constituents: Glycosides: These complex sugars help reduce inflammation and fluid retention under the eyes, contributing to the reduction of eye bags.
Polysaccharides: These long chains of sugar molecules have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the skin, reducing puffiness and eye bags.
Vitamins A and E: These antioxidants help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the appearance of eye bags and promoting a more even skin tone.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high gel content, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Gel from Leaves: The clear gel from the inner part of Aloe vera leaves is extracted for its soothing and calming effects on puffy eyes.
Aloe Vera Extract from Leaves: The extract from the leaves is often used due to its high concentration of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants that help reduce puffiness and dark circles.
remedy_recipe: Choose fresh aloe vera leaves of 10-12 inches long with thick fleshy green skin.
Cut the aloe vera leaves into 2-inch pieces and remove the yellow sap carefully.
Soak 5-7 pieces of aloe vera in 2 cups of water for 2-3 hours.
Boil the soaked aloe vera mixture for 10-15 minutes on low heat.
Strain the decoction and discard the solids then let it cool completely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American witch hazel decoctions helps with eye bags because of its potent astringent properties that help to constrict blood vessels and reduce puffiness. The decoction's anti-inflammatory compounds also soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and discoloration. Additionally, the natural astringency of witch hazel helps to dry out excess fluid retention beneath the eyes, leaving the area looking smoother and more refreshed.
remedy_properties: Astringent property: Witch hazel's astringent property helps to reduce puffiness and swelling under the eyes by constricting blood vessels and tightening the skin.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in witch hazel, such as gallic acid and hamamelitannin, help to reduce redness, swelling, and inflammation associated with eye bags and dark circles.

Antioxidant property: Witch hazel's antioxidant properties help to protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to signs of aging and discoloration.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: Tannins help reduce puffiness and inflammation by constricting blood vessels, thereby reducing the appearance of eye bags.
Gallic acid: Gallic acid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help combat free radicals that can cause inflammation and fluid retention, contributing to eye bags.
Hamamelitannin: Hamamelitannin has astringent properties, which can help tighten and firm the skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of eye bags and dark circles.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that help reduce puffiness and soothe the skin.
Buds: Employed for their ability to constrict blood vessels and reduce swelling, making them effective in reducing eye bag appearance.
Roots: Utilized for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce puffiness and promote overall skin health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 2 cups of dried hamamelis virginiana leaves and twigs from wild or cultivated sources.
Chop the dried material into smaller pieces and store in an airtight container for up to a year.
Combine 1 teaspoon of chopped hamamelis virginiana with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 10 minutes then strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve.
Discard the solids and store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 2 days or freeze for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with eye bags because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce puffiness and dark circles. The decoction's diuretic properties help to eliminate excess fluid retention, thereby reducing the appearance of swollen eyes. Additionally, dandelion's natural astringent properties constrict blood vessels, which can help to diminish the appearance of blue or purple undertones under the eyes. As a result, dandelion decoctions can provide a natural and effective solution for reducing the look of eye bags.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Dandelion's astringent compounds help to constrict blood vessels and reduce puffiness, making it effective in reducing the appearance of eye bags.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and terpenoids present in dandelion decoctions have anti-inflammatory effects that can help to soothe and calm irritated or inflamed eye tissues, alleviating puffiness and dark circles.
Diuretic properties: Dandelion's diuretic properties can help to reduce water retention and fluid buildup under the eyes, which can contribute to the appearance of eye bags.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help reduce inflammation and improve circulation, which can help alleviate puffiness and reduce the appearance of eye bags.
Taraxasterol: A triterpenoid saponin found in dandelion, taraxasterol has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and soothe the skin, making it beneficial for eye bags.

Chlorogenic acid: A type of polyphenol, chlorogenic acid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce water retention and improve lymphatic drainage, contributing to a reduction in eye bag appearance.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used for their diuretic properties, helping to reduce fluid retention and alleviate eye bag symptoms.
Roots: The roots are used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce puffiness and soothe the skin.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help to calm and reduce the appearance of eye bags.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 cups of fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and roots in the early morning.
Chop the harvested taraxacum officinale into small pieces to increase surface area for infusion.
Combine 2 cups of water with 1-2 tablespoons of chopped taraxacum officinale in a saucepan.
Boil the mixture for 10-15 minutes to release the active compounds into the water.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup or container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Highbush cranberry decoctions helps with eye bags because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The decoction's rich flavonoid content constricts blood vessels, reducing puffiness and dark circles under the eyes. Additionally, highbush cranberry's astringent properties help to tighten skin, diminishing the appearance of sagging or loose skin that can accentuate eye bags. Regular consumption of highbush cranberry decoctions can leave you with brighter, more radiant-looking eyes, banishing the look of tired and puffy eyes for good.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Highbush cranberry decoction contains antioxidants that help reduce puffiness and dark circles under the eyes by neutralizing free radicals and preventing oxidative damage.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The decoction's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help reduce swelling and soothe irritated skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of eye bags.

Vasoconstrictive properties: Highbush cranberry decoction may help constrict blood vessels under the eyes, which can reduce fluid accumulation and puffiness, leading to a more rested and refreshed look.
remedy_constituents: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help reduce puffiness and swelling around the eyes by neutralizing free radicals and promoting blood circulation.
Quercetin: As a flavonoid with potent anti-inflammatory properties, quercetin can help reduce eye bag swelling by minimizing inflammation and oxidative stress in the skin.
Catechins: These polyphenolic compounds can help reduce eye bags by promoting the breakdown of excess fluids and reducing inflammation, thereby reducing puffiness and promoting a more even skin tone.
remedy_parts: Fruits: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Leaves: They are used because of their astringent and anti-inflammatory effects.
Buds: They are used due to their ability to reduce puffiness and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Measure 2-4 teaspoons of dried vaccinium macrocarpon per 1 quart of water for decoction.
Combine measured vaccinium macrocarpon with water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to medium-low and let vaccinium macrocarpon simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Allow the decoction to cool before refrigerating and storing it in the refrigerator.

The decoction's flavonoids and phenolic acids work to reduce puffiness and dark circles by improving circulation and relaxing swollen tissues. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Damask rose decoctions have anti-inflammatory compounds like gallic acid, which helps reduce puffiness and swelling caused by eye bags.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in damask rose decoctions, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the appearance of dark circles and eye bags.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa damascena flowers at dawn to capture their peak aromatic properties and potency.
Combine 2 teaspoons of dried rosa damascena flowers with 1 quart of boiling water to create a strong infusion.
Steep the mixture for 10 to 15 minutes allowing the flavors and properties to fully infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids and achieve clarity.
Store the prepared herbal decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days to preserve its potency and freshness.

remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used to make decoctions for eye bags because they contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds that help to reduce puffiness and inflammation.
Roots: The roots are used to make decoctions for eye bags because they contain a rich source of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to soothe and calm the eye area.

Flowers: The flowers are used to make decoctions for eye bags because they contain a high concentration of flavonoids and other active compounds that help to reduce swelling and improve circulation in the eye area.
remedy_recipe: Gather 10 grams of dried euphrasia officinalis flowers and place them in a clean glass container.
Measure 200 milliliters of boiling water and slowly pour it over the euphrasia officinalis flowers.
Allow the mixture to steep for 15 to 20 minutes or until it reaches the desired temperature.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another container.
Discard the solids and store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey decoctions helps with eye bags because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that reduce puffiness and swelling. The herb's allantoin content also promotes collagen production, which firms and lifts the skin around the eyes. As a result, comfrey decoctions can help to diminish the appearance of dark circles and leave the under-eye area looking smoother and more radiant. Antioxidants: Comfrey's antioxidant properties help to combat free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to delicate skin tissues around the eyes, reducing the appearance of dark circles and eye bags.

Wound-healing agents: Comfrey's wound-healing compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, promote tissue repair and regeneration, which can help to reduce the appearance of under-eye discoloration and puffiness caused by skin damage or fatigue.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: Alkaloids present in Symphytum officinale, such as reticuline and allocryptopine, may help reduce puffiness and dark circles around the eyes by constricting blood vessels and improving circulation.

Stems: They are used to make decoctions for eye bags due to their content of mucilages, which can help soothe and calm irritated skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh or dried roots of symphytum officinale in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Clean and chop 2-4 grams of the harvested roots into small pieces for easier decoction.
Combine the chopped roots with 250 milliliters of boiling water in a saucepan or teapot.
Allow the mixture to simmer for 5-10 minutes to facilitate the extraction of active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile decoctions helps with eye bags because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The antioxidants present in chamomile tea, such as apigenin and luteolin, soothe and calm the skin around the eyes, reducing puffiness and dark circles. Additionally, chamomile's natural astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels, reducing swelling and discoloration. As a result, chamomile decoctions can provide a refreshing and rejuvenating solution for eye bags, leaving the under-eye area looking smooth, calm, and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, reduce puffiness and swelling associated with eye bags by calming irritated tissues and reducing inflammation.

Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the delicate skin around the eyes, helping to prevent dark circles and puffy eyes.

remedy_recipe: Rinse dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in cold water and drain well for optimal potency.
Measure 2 teaspoons of the dried flowers and add it to a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Heat filtered water to 212°f and pour it over the herbs allowing them to steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another cup discarding the solids completely.
Let the decoction cool slightly before using it as an eyewash by applying two drops twice daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort decoctions helps with eye bags because they contain hyperforin, a bioactive compound that has potent anti-inflammatory properties. When used as an eye compress or wash, this herbal extract can reduce puffiness and dark circles under the eyes by constricting blood vessels and decreasing fluid retention. Additionally, St John's Wort's antioxidant and anti-edematous effects help to soothe and calm the delicate skin around the eyes, leaving it looking smoother and more refreshed.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: St. John's Wort decoctions contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the skin, which can contribute to eye bags by reducing puffiness and dark circles.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in St. John's Wort decoctions can help reduce swelling and redness around the eyes, which are common symptoms of eye bags.

Vasoconstrictive properties: The flavonoids and hypericin present in St. John's Wort decoctions have been shown to constrict blood vessels, which can help reduce dark circles and puffiness under the eyes by reducing blood flow to the area.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.
Seeds: High in fatty acids and antioxidants, which soothe and moisturize the skin around the eyes, reducing fine lines and wrinkles.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh st john's wort leaves and flowers, weighing about one ounce for every four cups of water.
Combine the herb mixture with boiling water in a heat-resistant container, allowing it to steep for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.
Store the decoction in the refrigerator at 39°f to 46°f for up to three days, shaking well before use.
Use the decoction as an eye wash by gently pouring a few drops into each affected eye, closing the eyes and blinking several times.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel decoctions helps with eye bags because it has a unique ability to reduce puffiness and dark circles under the eyes. The antioxidants present in fennel help to combat inflammation and swelling, while its diuretic properties work to eliminate excess water retention. Additionally, fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds constrict blood vessels, reducing their appearance. By consuming fennel decoctions regularly, individuals can experience a noticeable reduction in eye bags and dark circles, leaving their eyes looking brighter, smoother, and more radiant.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Fennel's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce puffiness and swelling under the eyes, alleviating dark circles and eye bags.
Antioxidant: Fennel's antioxidant properties combat oxidative stress and free radicals that can cause premature aging and discoloration around the eyes, reducing the appearance of eye bags.
Diuretic: Fennel's diuretic properties help to reduce water retention and fluid accumulation under the eyes, further alleviating puffiness and dark circles.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing puffiness and soothing irritated eyes.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain a natural antiseptic that helps to calm and clean the eyes, reducing redness and itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried foeniculum vulgare, two cups of boiling water, and a clean glass jar.
Steep the dried foeniculum vulgare in boiling water for 10-15 minutes, then strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve.
Combine one cup of the cooled decoction with equal parts of lukewarm water to create a diluted solution for eye application.
Soak two cotton pads in the diluted solution and apply them over closed eyes for 5-10 minutes, then gently remove with a gentle stroking motion.
Refrigerate any remaining decoction for up to three days or freeze it for later use, making sure to label the container with date and contents.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle decoctions helps with eye bags because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce puffiness and soothe irritated skin. Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in stinging nettle decoctions neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the skin around the eyes, leading to dark circles and bags under the eyes.

Vasoconstrictive properties: Stinging nettle decoctions contain compounds that help constrict blood vessels, reducing the appearance of dark circles and puffiness under the eyes by minimizing fluid accumulation and congestion.

Buds: The tender buds of the nettle plant contain high concentrations of bioactive compounds that can help to alleviate dark circles and eye fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh urtica dioica leaves and stems according to local regulations and guidelines for sustainable harvesting.
Rinse the harvested plant material under cold running water to remove dirt and debris then pat dry.
Combine one teaspoon of dried or two teaspoons of fresh urtica dioica leaves and stems with 250 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes before straining the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle discarding solids.
Store the resulting decoction in the refrigerator and use within 24 hours as a compress applied chilled to reduce puffiness under the eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender decoctions helps with eye bags because it possesses anti-inflammatory properties that reduce puffiness and swelling around the eyes. supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eye bags that are treated with herbal decoctions are dark circles, puffy eyelids, and facial puffiness. Herbs like ashwagandha, chamomile, and peppermint are used in decoctions to reduce inflammation, improve circulation, and tighten skin tissues. Additionally, they may help soothe itchy or irritated skin around the eyes.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal decoctions that help with eye bags is a blend of chamomile, peppermint, and rose petals. Chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm the skin, reducing puffiness and redness. Peppermint's cooling effects help constrict blood vessels, minimizing fluid accumulation. Rose petals add an antioxidant boost, protecting the delicate skin around the eyes from environmental stressors and promoting overall eye health. Drinking these decoctions or applying them as a compress can help reduce eye bags and leave the skin feeling refreshed.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of the Indian Medical Association, a double masked placebo controlled multicentric randomised clinical trial was conducted in 157 patients suffering from various eye ailments, including eye bags, which can be a symptom of dry eye syndrome and ocular asthenia. The study found that the herbal eye drop preparation was significantly better than placebo in treating dry eye syndrome and ocular asthenia, reducing the severity of eye bags. By improving the condition of the eyes, the herbal decoction can help alleviate eye bags, providing a natural and effective solution to this common problem.
